name: Checks Cashed
description_short: Check Cashing 1% is a Glendale, Arizona check-cashing service offering flat-rate cashing on all check types plus MoneyGram services for money orders and bill payments.
description_long:
Checks Cashed 1%, LLC operates as a check-cashing service in Glendale, Arizona, serving the Greater Phoenix area for over a decade. The business specializes in converting various types of checks into immediate cash at a fixed fee structure. Their service model centers on accessibility and speed, marketing themselves as willing to handle check types and customer situations that larger financial institutions may decline.

The company offers check cashing for an extensive range of check types including payroll checks, tax refunds, social security income, insurance payments, personal checks, business checks, settlement receipts, inheritance payments, lottery winnings, and money orders. Beyond check cashing, they function as an authorized MoneyGram agent, providing money order services, bill payment processing, and money transfers. They advertise no limits on check amounts, claiming to cash checks ranging from $100 to $100,000.

Check Cashing 1% differentiates itself through a transparent, simple fee structure: 1% of the check amount plus a flat $1.00 fee, which they claim is lower than competitors in the Phoenix area. They accept government-issued IDs, military IDs, and valid passports as identification. The company emphasizes speed, promising cash delivery within minutes, and positions itself as a lender of last resort—willing to serve customers and check types that traditional check-cashing competitors refuse.

While the transparent pricing and stated willingness to handle all situations are genuine strengths for customers with limited banking access, the 1% fee structure means higher-value checks incur significantly larger costs. No independent verification of their fee competitiveness is available, and the service is limited to a single Glendale location, restricting accessibility for the broader Phoenix area.
pros: Transparent, straightforward fee structure: 1% of check amount plus $1.00 flat fee with no hidden charges
Claims to accept checks up to $100,000 with no maximum limits
Fast cash access promised within minutes
Wide variety of check types accepted: payroll, tax refunds, social security, insurance, personal, business, settlement, inheritance, and lottery checks
Authorized MoneyGram agent offering money orders, bill payment, and money transfer services
Flexible ID acceptance including government IDs, military IDs, and passports
Claims over a decade of service to the Glendale area
cons: 1% fee structure means significantly higher absolute costs on large checks (e.g., $100 on a $10,000 check), despite claims of lowest rates
Single location limits accessibility; customers must travel to Glendale, Arizona
No online or remote check-cashing option; requires in-person visit
Claims of 'lowest rates in Phoenix' are unverified and subjective without third-party rate comparison
No information provided about hours of operation, processing times during peak hours, or hold periods
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked individuals in the Glendale area needing immediate cash from checks
People with personal or non-standard checks that traditional banks refuse to cash
Those requiring MoneyGram services (money orders, bill payments, money transfers)
Workers receiving payroll or government benefit checks who need same-day liquidity
diagnosis: Best for unbanked customers in the Glendale area needing rapid cash conversion of various check types, particularly those rejected by traditional banks. The main caveat is that while the fee structure appears straightforward, the 1% rate becomes expensive on larger checks, and the single physical location severely limits accessibility for wider Phoenix-area consumers.
